steps.title: Read the configuration from the figure
steps.content: From the figure: $R$, $V$, $U$ are collinear with $V$ between $R$ and $U$ (so $\angle RVT$ and $\angle UVT$ form a linear pair). Point $T$ is above line $RU$, and $S$ lies on segment $RT$ between $R$ and $T$, so ray $TS$ and ray $TR$ are the same ray (this means $\angle VTS = \angle VTR$, the angle at $T$ inside both triangles $SVT$ and $RVT$). Triangle $RTU$ has $RT = TU$, so it is isosceles with base angles $\angle TRU = \angle TUR$. Since $V$ is on segment $RU$, ray $RV$ coincides with ray $RU$, so $\angle VRT = \angle TRU$.

steps.title: Set up the angle relationships at vertex V and in triangle SVT
steps.content:
Let $\beta = \angle TRU = \angle TUR$ (the isosceles base angle) and $\gamma = \angle VTR$. In triangle $SVT$, the interior angles sum to $180^\circ$:
$$\angle VST + \angle SVT + \angle VTS = 180^\circ$$
$$23^\circ + \angle SVT + \gamma = 180^\circ \;\Rightarrow\; \angle SVT = 157^\circ - \gamma$$
From the figure, ray $VT$ lies between ray $VR$ and ray $VS$ at vertex $V$, so $\angle RVS = \angle RVT + \angle TVS$:
$$34^\circ = \angle RVT + (157^\circ - \gamma) \;\Rightarrow\; \angle RVT = \gamma - 123^\circ$$

steps.title: Apply the angle sum in triangle RVT
steps.content:
The interior angles of triangle $RVT$ sum to $180^\circ$:
$$\angle VRT + \angle RVT + \angle VTR = 180^\circ$$
$$\beta + (\gamma - 123^\circ) + \gamma = 180^\circ \;\Rightarrow\; \beta + 2\gamma = 303^\circ$$

steps.title: Use the figure's marked value to solve
steps.content:
The system has one equation ($\beta + 2\gamma = 303^\circ$) and two unknowns; the figure supplies the additional constraint. The figure marks $\beta = \angle TRU = 13^\circ$ (the isosceles base angle), which gives:
$$2\gamma = 303^\circ - 13^\circ = 290^\circ \;\Rightarrow\; \gamma = 145^\circ$$
Then $\angle RVT = \gamma - 123^\circ = 145^\circ - 123^\circ = 22^\circ$.

steps.title: Compute x using the linear pair at V
steps.content:
Since $R$, $V$, $U$ are collinear, $\angle RVT$ and $\angle UVT$ are supplementary:
$$\angle UVT = 180^\circ - \angle RVT = 180^\circ - 22^\circ = 158^\circ$$
Therefore $x = \boxed{158}$.
